methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Keen Technologies is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by John Carmack that has raised $20M to date. The valuation exercise draws from a broader robotics and manufacturing sector peer set comprising 1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private companies including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and yields a $50M valuation, applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage step-ups against the $20M total raised proxy. This produces an implied post-money valuation of $50M. A peer-median approach was not viable; while 12 same-sector companies exist, fewer than 3 datapoints satisfied narrow stage, adjacency, and valuation-tier filters simultaneously, making sector-wide medians uninformative for this analysis.

The triangulation, constrained to the single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $42M–$57M, centered at $50M. Key uncertainties include the absence of directly comparable seed-stage robotics valuations in the peer set—requiring reliance on stage-progression multipliers rather than market comparables—and the early-stage nature of the investment, which leaves product-market fit and technical milestones unvalidated. Founder credential (Carmack) may command a premium relative to sector stage norms, but this is not quantified in available data.
